'''''Star Wars: Lethal Alliance''''' is a third-person action-adventure [[Star Wars video games|video game]] that was released for the [[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PlayStation Portable]] and [[Wikipedia:Nintendo DS|Nintendo DS]] consoles on [[December 5]] and [[December 12]], [[2006]], respectively. The game follows the adventures of [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Rianna Saren]] and [[Gender/Legends|her]] [[Security droid/Legends|security droid]], [[Z-58-0|Zeeo]], who attempt to [[Thief/Legends|steal]] the [[Death Star plans/Legends|Death Star blueprints]] and fight the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaver]] [[Zarien Kheev|Kheev]]. The game takes place just a few months before [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']].

==Opening crawl==
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=LETHAL ALLIANCE
|text=The Galactic Empire has
seized control of the
galaxy. Imperial forces
have imposed martial law
on countless planets, ruling
through fear and intimidation.

Governments do not resist,
fearing the Emperor's
wrath. But secretly, a Rebel
Alliance is forming.

On the city planet of
Coruscant, the mercenary
Rianna Saren wages her
own battle against the
Imperials...}}


==Plot summary==
A few months before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], [[Rianna Saren]], a [[Twi'lek]], started her life of freedom as a mercenary. Rianna had an arrogant streak due to her painful past as a slave. Rianna lost a [[Lekku/Legends|lekku]] after [[Zarien Kheev]], the [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] slaver who owned her, got angry at her for trying to escape and had her lekku cut off. Rianna eventually had a prosthetic replacement fitted. Rianna started off her mercenary career on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], but she often had to flee from Imperial scout groups.

[[File:LethalTatooine.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Rianna fighting stormtroopers on Tatooine]]
Saren eventually was contacted by [[Kyle Katarn]], who asked her to steal an Imperial shipment of [[Mirkanite/Legends|Mirkanite]] from a [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] warehouse. She decided to do it, for a price. She infiltrated the innermost area of the warehouse and killed many Black Sun guards, but was captured and accidentally activated [[Z-58-0|Zeeo]]. Zeeo freed her from her cell, and they acquired the Imperial shipment and brought it to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]]. [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] was very grateful and asked Rianna if she would go to [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. Rianna agreed and successfully shut down an Imperial drug chain. On Alderaan she confronted and killed [[Slak Sagar]], then took his [[DL-44 heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster]]. While on Alderaan she learned from [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] Leia that shipments of the drug were on [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]], where it was being used to increase the slave production of Mirkanite. Rianna set out to stop production at the factory.

[[File:LethalAlliance.jpg|thumb|250px|Rianna and Zeeo look upon the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star I]]]]
She infiltrated the factory and found some [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] slaves. The leader of the group said where the drill was in the factory. She deactivated the drill shaft and set off explosives to destroy the entire facility. She then came across Kheev and followed him to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], where her ship was shot down by [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] in the [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']]. While on Tatooine she located and killed the Twi'lek slaver [[Sedriss]] after finding out where Kheev was located and took his [[E-11 blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]]. He was in another part of the town, and when she found him in an arena he let a [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]] loose. Rianna Saren and Zeeo killed the rancor. Kheev then captured her and took her to [[Despayre]], where she found out about the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]].

Rianna escaped the prison on Despayre and made her way onto the Death Star before Despayre's destruction. Upon arrival on the Death Star, she headed to the [[Communication center]] and told the Rebels about the battlestation. She then stole an Imperial ship and headed to [[Danuta]], where the [[Death Star plans/Legends|plans of the Death Star]] were being held by Kheev. Rianna fought her way to Kheev and killed him in his personal battle armor, though not before Kheev took a pot shot at Rianna. Zeeo sacrificially took the shot and was seriously damaged. Rianna took the Death Star plans from Kheev's body.

Rianna, holding Zeeo, walked to her transport ready to continue her mercenary work.

==Gameplay==
[[File:Lethal_Alliance_Rancor.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Rianna fighting a rancor]]
Throughout the game, players are confronted by enemies, whom they must fight cooperatively, controlling both Rianna and her droid [[Z-58-0|Zeeo]] to accomplish their missions. Players are able to [[slicer/Legends|slice]] consoles, and after solving minigames, they can unlock doors, deactivate force fields, or download data. They can command [[Turbolaser/Legends|gun turrets]], climb walls and celings, while Zeeo provides [[deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]], explosives to force doors open, or sets up [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] disguises to fool [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Imperial Royal Guards]]. A multiplayer mode allows players to combat each other using droids from the same class as Zeeo.<ref name="Game">''Star Wars: Lethal Alliance''</ref>

There are significant differences between Nintendo DS and PlayStation Portable (PSP) versions, both in plot details, and in gameplay. The DS version encourages the player to use combos with Zeeo against enemies, in order to boost "Alliance" points, until they reach "Maximum Alliance" status; they the player is able to use a unique "Dual Cannon" attack by holding the X button.<ref name="Game DS">''Star Wars: Lethal Alliance'' on DS</ref> The PSP version has holographic disguises.<ref name="Game PSP">''Star Wars: Lethal Alliance'' on PSP</ref>
{{Clear}}

==Continuity==
The game [[retcon]]s or contradicts the [[1995]] PC game ''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'', where the Death Star plans are stolen by [[Kyle Katarn]] on [[Danuta]]. The game states that the plans that appear there are the updated plans that the architects on Danuta requested. 

However it rewrites significantly the status of Katarn, as in his backstory as already established, he was never a full member of the Alliance, and [[Jan Ors]] had to persuade [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] to recruit him for the mission as a mercenary.<ref name="sfte">''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]''</ref><ref>''[[Dark Forces Manual: Coded Transmissions]]''</ref> Yet in the first cutscene he seems already a high-regarded and trusted agent of the Alliance, who recuirts Saren and brings her to [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. 

In the custscenes, Katarn has his bearded appearance, as seen first in [[New Republic era]] publications (''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' and later). Later illustrations confirmed that Katarn already had this appearance around the time of the Danuta mission.<ref>''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref><ref name="sfte" />
